name: First Exchange Bank
description_short: Community bank serving North-Central West Virginia with 7 locations, offering checking, savings, CDs, mortgages, and personal loans with competitive rates.
description_long:
First Exchange Bank is a community-focused financial institution serving North-Central West Virginia with a strong local presence. The bank has been recognized as the Best Bank in Marion County as of 2025, indicating its reputation within its primary service region. Founded on principles of delivering exceptional personal and financial services, First Exchange Bank operates as a traditional brick-and-mortar community bank with both in-person and digital banking capabilities.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), personal loans, mortgages, and online banking services. Their CD offerings are particularly competitive, with rates ranging from 2.32% APY on 36-month CDs to 3.75% APY on 9-month and 12-month special CDs. Customers can open deposit accounts online and apply for mortgages through their web platform. The bank maintains 7 physical locations across the region with drive-thru ATMs at each branch and extended hours for customer convenience.

What distinguishes First Exchange Bank is its hyperlocal focus on a specific geographic region rather than national reach. The bank emphasizes community partnership and personal relationships, using language like "in real life" to contrast with larger national banks. They offer FDIC-insured CDs with flexible maturity options ranging from 91 days to 72 months and low minimum opening deposits of $500. The White Hall location features a Smart ATM that accepts both cash and check deposits, and the bank provides free mobile banking to customers.

As a regional community bank, First Exchange Bank's primary limitation is geographic scope—they serve only North-Central West Virginia, making them inaccessible to customers outside this region. While their CD rates are competitive for early 2026, they remain subject to market fluctuations. The bank's online banking capabilities exist but appear relatively basic compared to national digital-first competitors. For customers within their service area seeking traditional banking with local decision-making and personal service, they represent a solid option; for those requiring national reach or advanced digital features, larger institutions may be more appropriate. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Checking accounts with online account opening
Savings accounts with online account opening
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) with rates from 2.32% to 3.75% APY and terms from 91 days to 72 months
Personal loans with online application capability
Mortgage loans with online application and processing
Online banking accessible from any location
Drive-thru ATM access at all 7 branch locations
Smart ATM with cash and check deposit capability (White Hall location)
After-hours lost or stolen ATM/debit card support via hotline
pros: Competitive CD rates up to 3.75% APY with flexible maturity options from 91 days to 72 months
7 conveniently located branches across North-Central West Virginia with drive-thru access at each location
Smart ATM at White Hall location accepts both cash and check deposits for added convenience
Can open deposit accounts and apply for mortgages entirely online without visiting a branch
Free mobile banking with no fees charged by the bank itself
FDIC-insured deposits with low CD minimum opening balance of $500
Voted Best Bank in Marion County 2025, indicating strong local reputation and customer satisfaction
Extended hours including Saturday lobby hours (9am-12pm) at most locations
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only North-Central West Virginia, making it inaccessible to customers outside this region
Limited digital infrastructure compared to national banks and fintech competitors
No mention of business banking products, investment services, or wealth management on homepage
Relatively small institution with only 7 branches, limiting ATM network and service capacity
CD rates, while competitive, are variable and subject to market changes with no rate-lock guarantees mentioned
best_for: West Virginia residents in Marion, Monongalia, and Harrison counties seeking community banking with local decision-making
Customers prioritizing personal relationships and in-person service over digital-first banking experiences
Savers looking for competitive CD rates with flexible terms and low minimum deposit requirements
Homebuyers and mortgage applicants in North-Central West Virginia who prefer working with a local lender
diagnosis: First Exchange Bank is best suited for North-Central West Virginia residents who value community banking, personalized service, and competitive rates over digital convenience or national reach. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—the bank operates only in a specific region of West Virginia, making it unavailable to customers outside North-Central WV regardless of product quality.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
